rationale: The citing paper uses Baumgartner and Steenkamp (2001) to define response styles and to frame prior knowledge about systematic survey-response bias in marketing research. It then positions the anchor contraction effect as a new language-based source of response-style distortion in international settings, and explicitly states in the discussion that it adds to the response-styles literature including this work. This is more than a passing mention because the cited paper helps structure the conceptual setup and contribution claim, even though its specific models are not directly reused.
relation_description: The citing paper extends the response-style line of work represented by Baumgartner and Steenkamp (2001) by introducing and testing a new determinant of response bias: using L2 versus L1 emotional scale anchors. It builds on that prior framing of cross-national measurement bias to propose and validate ACE as an additional response-style mechanism.
